name: Turner Layton
abstract: Turner Layton (born John Turner Layton, Jr.; July 2, 1894 – February 6, 1978) was an American singer, songwriter and pianist. He frequently worked with Henry Creamer.

concerts.label: Benefit: Circle for Negro War Relief

works_created.t: I Took My Mother's Heart in My Two Hands
Way Down Yonder in New Orleans

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: Henry Creamer had written ... along with Turner Layton. See this was a -- Turner Layton and Henry Creamer had broken up now.
